old_value: {"id":"project-LICHEN-2auf.44.9.7","title":"Align DAO Path Sequence parent changes","description":"Current hardened Rust accepts a distinct parent at equal Path Sequence. RFC 6550 requires Path Sequence advancement when the parent set or lifetime changes. Reject equal-sequence additions/lifetime refresh while preserving idempotent copies, atomic grouped behavior, withdrawal, and multiparent updates carried by a newer sequence.","acceptance_criteria":"Equal Path Sequence is idempotent only; parent/lifetime changes require a newer sequence; grouped multiparent, stale, expiry, withdrawal, and replay tests pass in all implementations.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-18T20:37:41.253126Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-18T20:37:41.253126Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"project-LICHEN-2auf.44.9.7","depends_on_id":"project-LICHEN-2auf.44.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-07-18T20:37:41.392165Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","metadata":"{}"}]}
new_value: {"acceptance_criteria":"Equal Path Sequence is idempotent or adds a consistent simultaneous distinct candidate from the same sequence authority; existing candidate/lifetime/Path Control changes require a newer sequence; grouped multiparent, stale, expiry, withdrawal, and replay tests pass in all implementations.","description":"Current hardened Rust accepts any distinct parent at equal Path Sequence. RFC 6550 permits equal-sequence DAOs generated simultaneously for one Target, but not later mutation of installed candidate state. Accept a distinct equal-sequence candidate only when sequence authority, Path Lifetime, and Path Control are consistent; reject existing-candidate mutation, refresh, stale withdrawal, and ambiguous updates."}

new_value: {"acceptance_criteria":"A newer Path Sequence atomically installs the complete grouped candidate set; equal Path Sequence accepts only an exact idempotent copy; parent/lifetime/Path Control changes require a newer sequence; stale, expiry, withdrawal, replay, and capacity tests pass in all implementations.","description":"LICHEN requires the complete redundant candidate set for one Target and Path Sequence to be packed into one atomic DAO. Align Rust with that profile: equal Path Sequence is exact idempotent replay only; reject later candidate additions, existing-candidate mutation, lifetime/Path Control refresh, stale withdrawal, and ambiguous updates."}

new_value: {"notes":"Completed cross-implementation DAO atomic snapshot semantics in Rust, Python, and Zephyr C. Final corpus has 47 independently generated/validated vectors, including protected grouped tombstone reclamation and strict reserved-base/unsupported-option rejection. Gates: Rust lichen-rpl 149 tests, lichen-node 188 tests, no-default checks and Clippy; Python RPL/vector 307 passed with known blocked signed_encrypted link vector deselected, Ruff and strict MyPy on changed modules; Zephyr native_sim/native/64 18/18; generated fixture and git diff checks clean. Three fresh independent final reviews found no actionable P0-P4 defects. Residual: hardware/TSAN not run; authenticated C ingress remains separately blocked by project-LICHEN-2auf.44.9.10."}
Implemented and validated across Rust, Python, C, and canonical vectors
